executive_summary: Cross-diagram incident affecting 2 topology domain(s) (wan_topology, app_db_topology). 3 alert event(s) detected. Primary suspected root cause: WAN-PE-01 in wan_topology. Immediate triage recommended to validate and restore service.
evidence_from_graph: Alert propagation path: WAN-PE-01 → WAN-PE-02 → APP-LB-01
Retrieved SOP evidence: KB-SOP-WAN-001-001 — WAN Provider Edge Link Down and High Error Rate Response (Trigger Symptoms)
Retrieved SOP evidence: KB-SOP-WAN-001-000 — WAN Provider Edge Link Down and High Error Rate Response (Purpose)
Retrieved SOP evidence: KB-SOP-WAN-001-003 — WAN Provider Edge Link Down and High Error Rate Response (Pre-Checks)
CE-001 (temporal_correlation, confidence=1.0): 3 alert(s) within a 5-minute window (t=0..5 min), consistent with a propagating fault.

CE-002 (topology_proximity, confidence=0.4333): Alerted nodes (WAN-PE-01, WAN-PE-02, APP-LB-01) are topologically proximate (topology score 0.43), suggesting fault propagation through connected infrastructure.

CE-003 (cross_diagram_correlation, confidence=0.6): Events span 2 diagrams (app_db_topology, wan_topology). Cross-diagram correlation score 0.60 indicates the fault may have propagated across topology boundaries.
CE-004 (propagation_hypothesis, confidence=0.7167): Hypothesis: fault originated at the cluster seed node and may have propagated downstream through connected infrastructure.
Correlation reason: Events span 2 diagrams: app_db_topology, wan_topology
pre_checks: Confirm root-cause candidate WAN-PE-01 is present in enterprise graph memory.
Validate impacted diagrams before any change: wan_topology, app_db_topology.
Confirm RCA source and alert timeline freshness before remediation.
Run read-only reachability checks across diagram boundaries first.
triage_steps: Focus initial investigation on node 'WAN-PE-01' in wan_topology.
Verify BGP session state on WAN edge routers.
Check MPLS/carrier circuit health via NOC portal or vendor dashboard.
Review WAN path utilisation: confirm no QoS policy is dropping traffic.
Confirm backup path is available and not also degraded.
validation_steps: Confirm BGP session state shows Established on all WAN edge nodes.
Verify end-to-end reachability across the WAN link from multiple test points.
Confirm packet loss and error rate on the WAN interface have dropped to zero.
Verify carrier circuit status shows up in the NOC portal.
Confirm health of all impacted nodes: WAN-PE-01, WAN-PE-02, APP-LB-01.
remediation_steps: Verify WAN circuit status with the carrier via the NOC portal or vendor dashboard.
Inspect physical layer: check SFP module status and optic power levels if accessible.
Confirm BGP session state on WAN edge nodes — look for session reset timestamps.
If BGP is down, verify route advertisements are propagating after re-establishment.
Confirm the backup WAN path is active and carrying traffic during the outage.
Document the change and update the CMDB record for the affected WAN edge node.
Follow SOP-WAN-001 (WAN Provider Edge Link Down and High Error Rate Response) remediation section [KB-SOP-WAN-001-000].
post_checks: Confirm BGP session state shows Established on all WAN edge nodes.
Verify end-to-end reachability across the WAN link from multiple test points.
Confirm packet loss and error rate on the WAN interface have dropped to zero.
Verify carrier circuit status shows up in the NOC portal.
Confirm health of all impacted nodes: WAN-PE-01, WAN-PE-02, APP-LB-01.
do_not_execute_if: Do not execute changes if root cause WAN-PE-01 is not confirmed by graph evidence.
Do not execute if a rollback owner and maintenance window are not approved.
Do not execute if alert timestamps are stale or no longer match the active incident.
Do not execute cross-domain changes without NOC and owning team approval.
rollback_or_safety_notes: Capture running configs on all affected devices before any change.
Coordinate maintenance windows across affected sites — changes here have cross-domain blast radius.
Confirm a rollback plan is in place for each affected diagram domain.
Notify NOC before modifying WAN or shared-services components.
Stage changes in the least-critical diagram first; validate before proceeding.
escalation_recommendation: Escalate to network engineering team if root-cause node cannot be restored within 30 minutes, or if the incident spans more than 3 topology domains. Notify on-call SRE if customer-facing services remain impacted.
